projectDescription: A visual desktop application for exploring and editing WIM, ESD, and SWM Windows image files using wimlib-imagex as its engine.
keyFeatures: Visual file explorer with folder tree, content table, and breadcrumb navigation
Open and browse WIM, ESD, and SWM image files
Multi-image selector for WIM files with multiple internal images
Extract files and folders to any destination on the system
Add files or folders via buttons or drag-and-drop
Replace existing files inside WIM images
Delete content with visual confirmation dialog
Conflict detection before importing to prevent accidental overwrites
Integrated wimlib-imagex terminal for advanced commands
7-Zip integration for enriched file metadata (size, date)
System tray integration for quick access and controlled shutdown
Intelligent launcher that auto-installs Node.js and dependencies
publicApiSurface: POST /api/open — Open a WIM/ESD/SWM file and read image metadata
GET /api/browse — List contents of the current image (7z + wimlib fallback)
POST /api/extract — Extract selected files/folders to a destination directory
POST /api/add — Upload and add files to the current WIM image
POST /api/delete — Delete files or folders from the current WIM image
POST /api/replace — Replace a file in the WIM with an uploaded file
POST /api/batch-import — Batch import files with server-side conflict revalidation
POST /api/check-conflicts — Check for path conflicts before importing
POST /api/wimlib-cmd — Execute raw wimlib-imagex commands via the terminal
GET /api/logs/stream — SSE endpoint for real-time log streaming
GET /api/status — Get current operation status and open WIM path
GET /api/wimlib-version — Get the bundled wimlib-imagex version
GET /api/7z-status — Get 7-Zip availability and path info
POST /api/7z-set-path — Set a custom 7-Zip directory path
GET /api/pick-file — Open native Windows file picker (PowerShell)
GET /api/pick-folder — Open native Windows folder picker (PowerShell)
POST /api/exit — Gracefully shut down the WimExplorer server
